# [Game Name] — Game Design Document

**Version**: 0.1 (Draft)
**Author**: [Your name]
**Date**: [Date]
**Status**: Draft

## 1. Overview

### Elevator Pitch
[1-2 sentences that make someone want to play this game]

### Genre
[e.g., 2D Platformer / Top-Down Action RPG / Puzzle / etc.]

### Platform
[PC / Mobile / Console / Multi-platform]

### Target Audience
[Age, experience level, what games they love]

## 2. Core Experience

### Core Fantasy
[What does the player get to BE or DO?]

### Design Pillars
See `design/pillars.md`

### Target Session Length
[How long is a typical play session?]

## 3. Core Mechanics

### Primary Mechanic
[The most important thing the player does]

**How it works**:
1. [Step]
2. [Step]

**Why it's fun**: [Player psychology — what makes this engaging?]

### Secondary Mechanics
[Supporting mechanics that enrich the primary]

## 4. Game Loop

### Core Loop (30-second cycle)
```
Player does [action] → World responds with [reaction] → Player gains [reward/progression]
```

### Session Loop (10-30 minute cycle)
[What a full play session looks like — start, middle, end]

### Progression Loop (multi-session)
[Long-term motivation — what keeps players coming back?]

## 5. Player Progression

### Progression Type
[Linear / Branching / Emergent / Sandbox]

### Progression Systems
[Levels, skills, items, unlocks — what grows over time?]

## 6. World and Setting

### Setting
[Brief description of the game world]

### Tone
[Whimsical / Gritty / Serious / Comedic / etc.]

## 7. Win/Lose Conditions

### Success State
[How does the player win, complete a level, or achieve a goal?]

### Failure State
[How does the player fail, and what happens?]
